explanations.7: The [[ ! -d ... ]] checks if a directory doesn't exist; if true, it prints an error message in red and exits the script early with return 0.
explanations.8: Similarly, this checks if the file doesn't exist in the source folder; the -f means 'is it a file' rather than a directory.
explanations.12: The \033[1;34m and \033[0m are ANSI escape codes that color text—blue for the message, then reset the color at the end.
explanations.13: This printf uses multiple color codes: \033[1;31m for red text and \033[1;32m for green text, to highlight which file is which in the output.
explanations.15: The pushd/popd commands change into the source folder, run diff to compare the files, then return to the original directory; diff shows only lines that differ between the two files.
